human_written_text: For ARM assembly for example, MOV, LDR, ADD/SUB, CMP, BNE, LSL/LSR gives you the basics for building almost any other highlevel abstraction. Sure there's more instructions (~50?) and a lot of optional variations, but almost everything boils down to those basic instructions
paraphrased_text: ARM assembly stuff includes some basic instructions like MOV, which moves data around, and LDR, which pulls in data, then you've got your arithmetic operations, like ADD and SUB, that handle simple math and number shenanigans to make coding way easier.
